## Summary

What changed and why?

## User problem

Which user workflow does this improve?

- [ ] First install or launch
- [ ] Collecting snippets
- [ ] Finding or filtering snippets
- [ ] Composing with blocks
- [ ] Pasting or dragging output
- [ ] AI-generated titles/tags
- [ ] Privacy, permissions, or local data handling
- [ ] Contributor or release workflow

## Product impact

- [ ] Improves the core collect/search/compose/paste workflow
- [ ] Improves reliability, privacy, or permissions
- [ ] Improves install, docs, or open-source onboarding
- [ ] Other

## Privacy and safety

- [ ] Clipboard content stays local by default
- [ ] No telemetry, accounts, or cloud sync were added
- [ ] AI/network behavior is opt-in and documented, if changed
- [ ] Diagnostics, logs, screenshots, and tests contain no private clipboard data or API keys
- [ ] Keychain, encrypted state, and attachment handling are unchanged or documented

## Checks

- [ ] `swift build`
- [ ] `swift test`
- [ ] `./Scripts/check-doc-links.sh` for documentation changes
- [ ] I did not commit `.build/`, API keys, local clipboard data, or private screenshots
- [ ] I updated docs when user-facing behavior changed
- [ ] I updated `CHANGELOG.md` for user-facing, documentation, release, or maintenance changes
- [ ] Labels match `.github/release.yml` when this should appear in release notes

## Screenshots

Add before/after screenshots for UI changes when useful. For README-ready assets, follow `docs/DEMO.md` and avoid private clipboard content.

## Release notes

One sentence a user could understand in release notes:
